item.title: Version 0.9.34
item.description:
v0.9.34 — richer Speakers Library + locked avatar color

  • Every utterance is a sample now. Open a speaker in the Library and you see every sentence they contributed across all sessions — newest first, each row playable with the correct time range. Used to be a single 5-second enrollment clip; now it's the real catalogue.
  • Real waveforms. The bar charts next to each sample are actual audio, not a hash of the row id pretending to be audio. Computed on the fly the first time you open a speaker (a subtle shimmer shows up while it works), then cached so re-opens are instant.
  • Avatar colors stay put on rename. Renaming "Yoni" → "Yoni Bloch" no longer shifts the hue across Library, Transcript Reader, Meeting Library, or menu-bar popover. Color locks to whatever you were seeing before the rename. Undo preserves it too.
  • "Me" identity is durable. If you ever rename your own voice profile to something other than your macOS login name, the "Me" labelling across transcripts keeps working correctly. Previously this silently broke.

item.title: Version 0.9.33
item.description:
v0.9.33 — you'll actually see update alerts now

Mini Granola runs as a menubar app with no windows most of the time. Sparkle's default background-update dialog was opening behind everything and quietly vanishing. This release implements Sparkle's gentle-reminders protocol: when an update is available, you'll get a proper macOS notification ("Mini Granola update available") with an Install button that brings the update dialog to the front. No more silently missing updates.

item.title: Version 0.9.32
item.description:
v0.9.32 — upgrade old sessions to word-level sync

  • Upgrade banner in the Transcript Reader. Old Soniox recordings that predate v0.9.31's exact word sync now show a one-line banner with a Re-process button — ~2 minutes per session, speaker labels preserved.

item.title: Version 0.9.31
item.description:
v0.9.31 — exact word-level sync in the Transcript Reader

  • The highlighted word now tracks the actual audio. Previously the reader interpolated word position linearly across each segment's duration. On long continuous speech (Hebrew Zoom calls with 15–21-second segments), the cursor drifted several words off by mid-segment. Now we preserve Soniox's per-token timestamps and binary-search for the exact token being spoken at the current playback time.
  • Backward-compat preserved — pre-v0.9.31 sessions fall back to the old linear scheme; new Soniox sessions get the exact sync. WhisperKit sessions are unchanged (no per-token timing available from that backend yet).

item.title: Version 0.9.30
item.description:
v0.9.30 — session repair + ghost rows + diagnostics

  • Speakers Library shows ghost rows — speakers whose profile was renamed or deleted now appear greyed-out ("unenrolled" or "label only") instead of silently vanishing. Fixes the "Speaker no longer exists" toast when you deep-link from the Transcript Reader's chip menu.
  • Me / real-name consolidation banner — older sessions that predate the v0.9.6 identity merge show both "Me" and your system name as separate speakers. The Speaker Editor now detects the split and offers a one-click Consolidate (with 5-second Undo) that rewrites the transcript to unify them. Voice store is untouched, so other sessions still read correctly.
  • Under the hood — pipeline.log now records top-3 similarity scores for every diarization cluster, for future threshold tuning. Settings → About → Match diagnostics has an Export button that writes a scrubbed NDJSON to your Downloads folder (scores only, no names, no transcripts, no audio).

item.title: Version 0.9.28
item.description:
v0.9.28 — sample-trust fix + icon re-render

  • Play sample plays the right audio — the Speaker Editor now picks longer, later sample turns so you hear the stable mid-session portion of a speaker's voice instead of an early mis-clustered segment.
  • Play tooltip shows the turn's text + timestamp on hover, so you can see what you'll hear before clicking.
  • Dock icon renders edge-to-edge — fixed the transparent border that caused macOS to show a grey canvas around the icon.

item.title: Version 0.9.27
item.description:
v0.9.27 — Speaker Editor inline tag row + fresh dock icon

Tagging unknown speakers got a big upgrade. Clicking the pencil on an unknown speaker expands an inline row with everything you need in one place.

  • Inline tag row — transcript quote + play sample + TextField + Skip + Save + up to 3 smart chips, all in one tidy block.
  • Smart chip recency — library suggestions sort by "most recently tagged across any session."
  • Implicit merge on exact match — typing a library user's name exactly routes to assign-to-library.
  • Skip session-state — skip all unknowns and the banner disappears with an Undo toast.
  • Tab / Shift-Tab navigate the unknown queue without leaving the keyboard.
  • Fresh dock icon — frosted-blue granola jar and wooden spoon.

item.title: Version 0.9.26
item.description:
v0.9.26 — cross-session rename / forget / merge (A2)

Rename, Forget, and Merge in the Speakers Library now change every past meeting where the speaker appears — not just the voice store.

  • Cross-session rename/forget/merge — cascade updates across transcripts + meeting titles.
  • 7-second undo with a ring-timer toast; everything rolls back byte-identical.
  • Smart confirmation — always ask before touching past meetings; "Don't ask again under 20 meetings" opt-in for small fixes.
  • Inline row progress while the cascade runs; other library rows stay interactive.
  • APFS copy-on-write snapshots for durable, free (~0 bytes) undo backups.

item.title: Version 0.9.25
item.description:
Speakers Library

The Speakers tab moves out of Settings into a standalone library window. Every known voice gets real real estate: hero card, last-heard grouping, full-width voice sample, and a visual fingerprint.

Getting there

  • Menubar popover → Speakers Library… (new row between Recent meetings and Settings).
  • ⌘⇧S from anywhere with Mini-Granola frontmost.
  • Right-click any speaker chip in a transcript → "Open in Speakers Library". The chip labeled "Me" resolves to your real voice profile (matched via your macOS full name).
  • Deep link minigranola://speakers/{id} for inter-app automation.

What's in the window

  • Left pane — search (⌘F to focus), filter chips (All / Recent), speakers grouped by last-heard recency (Heard today, Yesterday, This week, This month, Earlier).
  • Right pane — hero (avatar + name + "You" pill for your own profile + samples × meetings × minutes-heard meta), action bar (Rename, Forget voice), voice samples section, and a decorative voice fingerprint strip.

This ship keeps rename + forget single-profile — they update the voice store, same semantics as the old Settings tab. Past meeting transcripts keep their existing labels. v0.9.25-A2 (coming next) makes those cross-session: rename updates every past meeting's transcript + title, forget runs a 7-second-undo cascade across all sessions.

Cold-launch deep links now work — URLs that arrive before the app finishes launching queue up and fire once dependencies are wired.

The Settings → Speakers tab is deleted. All that functionality (rename, forget, play sample) is in the library window now.

item.title: Version 0.9.24.2
item.description:
Speaker Editor title sync

Follow-up to v0.9.24.1. The reader got its titlebar sync last release; the Speaker Editor had the same bug in two places — the NSWindow titlebar and the session header in the editor body both read the meeting name once at open and never refreshed. So renaming a speaker that changed the meeting title (e.g. SPEAKER_01 → "Gilad Bright") updated the transcript, the speaker chips, and the Meeting Library row, but left the editor's own chrome stuck on the old name until you closed and reopened the editor.

Both now track meta.meeting_name live, via the same pattern as the reader. Also audited the Meeting Library window — no bug there; its title is static and row/detail labels already refresh from RecentMeetingsStore.

item.title: Version 0.9.24.1
item.description:
Reader titlebar sync

Point release fixing a stale titlebar in the Transcript Reader. When v0.9.24's meeting-name propagation kicked in (renaming SPEAKER_01 → "Gilad Bright" updates the meeting title in meta.json), the reader's body header refreshed correctly but the macOS titlebar at the very top of the window stayed frozen at the original ingest-time name until you closed and reopened the reader.

Now the NSWindow titlebar also tracks meta.meeting_name and updates live when any edit from the Speaker Editor changes it.

item.title: Version 0.9.24
item.description:
Speaker Editor — full redesign

  • New Speaker Editor window — standalone 680×560 resizable window, reachable from the Meeting Library, the Transcript Reader toolbar, the menu-bar popover, and the post-recording HUD. Replaces the old 460×440 tagging card.
  • Focus mode for unknown speakers — one-at-a-time expanded card with XL avatar, 5-second audio sample, transcript excerpt, name field, and library suggestions (top 3 from your voice library, ranked by sample count). Save / Skip / "See all speakers" escape hatch.
  • Full-list mode for review — every speaker in the session with status pill (Me / Enrolled / Unknown), speaking-time bar, hover actions, and right-click menu (Rename, Assign to library, Merge, Mark as non-speech, Remove).
  • Suggestion chips — typing a name that matches someone in your library auto-assigns the existing voice profile instead of creating a new one.
  • Post-toast undo — the 5-second toast Undo now has a persistent sibling: an inline "Undo: {edit}" hint in the editor footer that stays until you revert or close. ⌘Z reverts the most recent speaker edit (LIFO; native text-undo in text fields is unaffected).

Non-speech segments are visible in the Reader

When you mark a speaker as non-speech, those segments no longer disappear — they render muted (faded, italic, [non-speech] prefix) with a speaker-slash avatar and a clean "Non-speech" label. Still excluded from the Markdown export and the speaker chip row. Easier to audit what you muted and un-mark anything you flagged by mistake.

Dock icon + Cmd-Tab on demand

Mini-Granola stays a menu-bar-only app at rest. As soon as you open any real window (Meeting Library, Transcript Reader, Speaker Editor, Settings), the app promotes: Dock icon appears, Cmd-Tab finds it, Mission Control sees its windows. Close the last window and it drops back to menu-bar-only.

Meeting titles update when you rename a speaker

Renaming SPEAKER_01 to "Gilad Bright" now propagates into the meeting name (Reader window title, Library row). Previously the segments updated but the title stayed frozen at the unresolved label.

item.title: Version 0.9.22
item.description:
New Meeting Library

  • Full-history browsable meeting library with search, 4 filter chips (All / This week / Month / Needs tagging), date-grouped list, and a detail pane with action buttons
  • Open it anytime with ⌘L, or via the menu-bar popover's new "See all →" link
  • Copy button in the detail pane puts the full markdown transcript on your clipboard
  • Import audio lives as a native button in the titlebar
  • Action row buttons: Open transcript, Tag speakers, Copy, Summarize (coming soon), Reveal in Finder

Copy, paste, select-all now work everywhere

Added a real macOS main menu, so ⌘C / ⌘V / ⌘A / ⌘X / ⌘Z finally work in every text field across the app. Previously these shortcuts had no handler because menu-bar-only apps don't get a menu by default.

Warm palette in dark mode

Dark-mode surfaces are now a proper warm dark-brown (#232120) with warm off-white text, matching the light-mode terracotta aesthetic. No more system-blue accent leaks or cool-gray backgrounds.

Reveal-in-Finder now selects the session folder in its parent window (instead of opening the folder itself) — matches what the menu item says.

item.title: Version 0.9.21
item.description:
Transcript reader: Copy button

Copy the full markdown transcript to your clipboard with one click — button lives at the top-right of the transcript window. Pasting into Slack, docs, or notes produces the same content the reader shows.

Also added a greyed-out Summarize button as a placeholder for an upcoming LLM-powered meeting-summary feature.

item.title: Version 0.9.20
item.description:
Transcript reader: ⌘W closes the window

The in-app transcript reader promised ⌘W as a close shortcut but the keystroke did nothing. Now works as expected — ⌘W closes the focused transcript window, same as clicking the red traffic-light button.

item.title: Version 0.9.19
item.description:
Google Meet & Microsoft Teams web auto-detection

  • Automatic meeting detection now covers Google Meet and Microsoft Teams web in Chrome, Arc, and Safari — no more recording only on Zoom.
  • Works regardless of which tab has focus — we enumerate every tab, so a Meet call in a background tab is still picked up.
  • Gated on the system-wide microphone-in-use signal (the same one lighting the orange mic dot in the menu bar), so stale tabs, lobby screens, and "created a link but didn't join" states don't trigger a false recording.
  • Faster time-to-first-detection: activating a browser now triggers a fast-poll burst, so meetings are picked up within ~9 seconds instead of up to 45.
  • Transcript-ready HUD no longer visually stacks with the macOS notification banner — banner goes to Notification Center only, HUD handles the on-screen CTA.

Zoom detection unchanged. Native Microsoft Teams app is still on the old heuristic — next up.

item.title: Version 0.9.16
item.description:
Mini Granola UI redesign

  • Floating HUD rewrite — app-themed icon block (Zoom/Meet/Slack/Teams), animated countdown bar, ⏎/esc kbd hints, "Transcript ready" HUD replaces silent banner notifications
  • Settings window — sidebar + seven tabs (Recording, Transcription, Speakers, Shortcuts, Calendar, AI Naming, About). API-key drafts persist across tab switches.
  • Tagging window — card layout with italic sample quote and animated waveform
  • In-app transcript reader — multi-window, proportional word highlighting during playback, 80-bar scrub waveform, smart auto-scroll (pauses while you scroll)
  • Light mode with new warm-terracotta palette. Settings → About → Appearance lets you pick Auto / Light / Dark.

Dark mode renders as before — the new palette only kicks in when the system is light.

item.title: Version 0.9.15
item.description:
Menu bar popover UI refresh

  • New SwiftUI popover replaces the old NSMenu — live recording cell with breathing waveform, REC pill, mono timer
  • Status-bar icon animates as a 5-bar waveform during recording
  • Recent meeting rows: left-click opens transcript, right-click for context menu, red badge opens Tag Speakers
  • Dark mode supported. Activity log moved to Settings.

Carries forward v0.9.14's voice re-check and SystemAudioBridge race fix.

item.title: Version 0.9.14
item.description:
Per-segment voice re-check + SystemAudioBridge race fix

  • Per-segment voice embedding re-check — catches longer (>0.5s) diarizer misattributions
  • Phase-2 race condition fix in SystemAudioBridge — replaced NSLock with a serial dispatch queue

item.title: Version 0.9.13
item.description: Mid-sentence diarization flicker fix

item.title: Version 0.9.12
item.description: Sparkle integration + Soniox streaming + audio hardening
